name: Rust Axum Thin Handler
summary: Keep Axum handlers thin by limiting them to extraction, use-case invocation, and response mapping.

rules: The handler should delegate behavior to an existing use case instead of implementing application decisions inline.
Request and response DTOs belong at the HTTP boundary.
If the application/use-case boundary does not already exist, do not create business behavior as part of the Axum adapter task; report the missing prerequisite.
checks: Handler code is mostly extraction, input construction, await, and response mapping.
The use-case function or trait does not depend on axum types.
promptFragments: When adding an Axum endpoint, first identify the existing use-case entry point and its input/output types, then adapt the HTTP request into that contract.

items: Find the existing use-case entry point and required dependencies.
Stop and report the missing prerequisite if no existing application/use-case boundary owns the behavior.
Add or reuse an application state accessor for those dependencies.
Define HTTP request and response DTOs in the transport layer.
Map request DTOs and route/path/query values into the use-case input.
Await the use case and map the result into a response DTO.
Register the route near related Axum route definitions.

blocks.reason: Thin handlers are the Axum implementation of the adapter pattern.
